response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a skilled polyglot engineer with solid TypeScript/Node.js and DLang experience, but the role requires deep JVM expertise with Java, Kotlin, Spring, and enterprise framework experience that is largely absent from his background. Java appears only briefly in embedded and tooling contexts at Altom, with no evidence of Spring, Hibernate, Quarkus, or Kotlin usage anywhere in his history. His time at National Instruments touched on IDE development, but that was C/C++ tooling rather than JVM-based IDE internals, and the tenure was short (six months). The technical core of this position does not align with his demonstrated stack.
strengths: Demonstrated software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) applicable to large-scale, quality-driven codebases
Prior direct experience contributing to an IDE product (NI LabWindows/CVI) shows at least surface-level familiarity with developer tooling challenges
Strong track record designing and maintaining developer-facing libraries and frameworks (test runners, assertion libraries, DAV support)
concerns: Kotlin is entirely absent from the resume — no mention in any role, project, or skills list
No Spring, Quarkus, or Hibernate experience; primary production stack is TypeScript/Node.js with DLang as a secondary language
JVM depth is undemonstrated — Java references are limited to brief, peripheral usage at Altom with no enterprise-scale or framework-level context
